zh.lifeSummary: 當James Fraser of Foyness, of Culbokie於大約1499年出生在英國蘇格蘭音巴內-爾時，他的父親Thomas Fraser 9th of Lovat, 2nd Lord Fraser of Lovat40歲，母親Janet-nic-Alexander Gordon of Midmar, Auchindown, Tulloch and Abergeldie27歲。他於大約1514年與Anna Sinclair在英國蘇格蘭羅斯 安德 可洛馬迪結婚。他們至少是4個女兒的父母。他去世於1544年7月15日在蘇格蘭音巴內-爾享年46歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当James Fraser of Foyness, of Culbokie于大约1499年出生在英国苏格兰音巴內-爾时，他的父亲Thomas Fraser 9th of Lovat, 2nd Lord Fraser of Lovat40岁，母亲Janet-nic-Alexander Gordon of Midmar, Auchindown, Tulloch and Abergeldie27岁。他于大约1514年与Anna Sinclair在英国苏格兰羅斯 安德 可洛馬迪结婚。他们至少是4个女儿的父母。他去世于1544年7月15日在苏格兰音巴內-爾享年46岁。

nameMeanings.name: Fraser
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: apparently a nickname from Anglo-Norman French fraser(e), fresere ‘strawberry plant’. Fresel and Frisel, from Anglo-Norman French fresel ‘strawberry’, are early variants of the Fraser surname, the modern Gaelicized form of which is Friseal. See Frizzell . The crest on the Scottish family's coat of arms takes the form of a strawberry plant, but its antiquity is unknown. Claims of a habitational derivation, in particular from a place called la Frézelière in Anjou (France), are attractive but they lack verifiable evidence.
Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.

History: There are two clans Fraser in Scotland, with common ancestry going back to Simon Fraser of Keith in East Lothian, who lived in the 12th century. One of these clans has its seat at Philorth Castle (subsequently re-named Cairnbulg) on the northeast coast of Scotland. Sir Alexander Fraser, 8th laird of Philorth (c. 1536–1623) converted the fishing village of Faithlie into the burgh of Fraserburgh in the 1590s. The other clan is Fraser of Lovat, associated mainly with the city of Inverness are. They are descended from Simon Fraser, a younger son, who lived in the early 14th century. In Gaelic, the head of Clan Fraser of Lovat is known as Mac Shimi ‘son of Simon’.

variants.name: Frayser
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Fraser  .

variants.name: Frasier
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Fraser  .

variants.name: Frazer
meanings: Variant of   Fraser  , associated chiefly with northern Ireland.

variants.name: Frashier
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Fraser  .

variants.name: Frizzell
meanings: English and Irish: nickname from Old French fresel, frisel ‘lace, ribbon, braid, brooch, frill, ruff’, for someone with an ornate style of dress, or ‘object of little value’ (a diminutive of frese, of ancient Germanic origin).
Scottish: from Anglo-Norman French fresel ‘strawberry’, an alternative form of Fraser .

variants.name: Frazier
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Fraser  .

variants.name: Frasher
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Fraser  .
Possibly an altered form of German Fratzscher (see Frutchey ).

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

en.lifeSummary: When James Fraser of Foyness, of Culbokie was born about 1499, in Inverness-shire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Thomas Fraser 9th of Lovat, 2nd Lord Fraser of Lovat, was 40 and his mother, Janet-nic-Alexander Gordon of Midmar, Auchindown, Tulloch and Abergeldie, was 27. He married Anna Sinclair about 1514, in Ross and Cromarty, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 daughters. He died on 15 July 1544, in Inverness-shire, Scotland, at the age of 46.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque James Fraser of Foyness, of Culbokie est né environ 1499, à Inverness-shire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas Fraser 9th of Lovat, 2nd Lord Fraser of Lovat, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Janet-nic-Alexander Gordon of Midmar, Auchindown, Tulloch and Abergeldie, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Anna Sinclair environ 1514, à Ross and Cromarty, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 filles. Il est décédé le 15 juillet 1544, à Inverness-shire, Écosse, à l’âge de 46 ans.

ko.Cromarty, Ross Cromarty, Scotland: 영국 스코틀랜드 로스 앤드 크로마티 크로마티
ko.lifeSummary: James Fraser of Foyness, of Culbokie(이)가 대략 1499년 영국 스코틀랜드 인버네스-실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Fraser 9th of Lovat, 2nd Lord Fraser of Lovat(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Janet-nic-Alexander Gordon of Midmar, Auchindown, Tulloch and Abergeldie(은)는 27세였다. 그는 대략 1514년 영국 스코틀랜드 로스 앤드 크로마티에서 Anna Sinclair(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1544년 7월 15일 스컷랜드 인버네스-실르에서 46세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Cromarty, Ross Cromarty, Scotland: кромерти, росс анд кромерти, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда James Fraser of Foyness, of Culbokie родился около 1499 г. в местности инвирнесс-шир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Thomas Fraser 9th of Lovat, 2nd Lord Fraser of Lovat было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Janet-nic-Alexander Gordon of Midmar, Auchindown, Tulloch and Abergeldie, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Anna Sinclair около 1514 г. в местности росс анд кромерти, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 дочери. Он умер 15 июля 1544 г. в местности инвирнесс-шир, Шотландия в возрасте 46 лет.

Se casó con Anna Sinclair aproximadamente 1514, en Ross and Cromarty, Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijas. Murió el 15 de julio de 1544, en Inverness-shire, Scotland, a la edad de 46 años.
